body_txt: Anarchism is an umbrella term for a bunch of views that advocate the reduction or elimination (or even simply ignoring) of hierarchic power and its replacement by various forms of voluntary non-hierarchical cooperation. Historically, many anarchists encourage nonviolent means for this goal. And then you have these guys , who are very much the reason why The Revolution Will Not Be Civilized . They're out to smash "The System" — especially the government , the elites , large corporations , and financial institutions — while anyone who gets in their way is at risk of becoming a necessary sacrifice in their war against tyranny . This will often end up including the entire civilian population , who are left helpless once the Bomb-Throwing Anarchists successfully bring down the "vile, oppressive State", having destroyed the institutions and infrastructure necessary for a functional modern society in the process . Due to The Horseshoe Effect , this means their end goals sometimes have a lot in common with those of the Right-Wing Militia Fanatic and the Evil Reactionary , despite technically being ideological opposites. This portrayal of anarchism has been around since the 19th century, as it first began to take form as a social movement, and intensified after a wave of anarchist assassinations, including bombings, of prominent heads of state and government in the late 19th and early 20th century. This led to newspapers, propaganda, and fiction demonizing anarchists as maniacs who simply want nothing but chaos , destruction , and anarchy for anarchy's sake , often filling the role of Terrorists Without a Cause — and consequently, often having little to no connection with actual anarchist beliefs . This also tended to be the stereotypical image of communists for many Westerners until the "Orwellian intellectual infiltrating the government" image gained popularity starting in the 1940s. Some later works have introduced more sympathetic Bomb-Throwing Anarchists, depicting them as Well Intentioned Extremists who are genuinely fighting against a cruel and repressive regime , but have ended up Jumping Off the Slippery Slope to become just as destructive as their enemies , who have been brutalized so respond with brutality in turn , or are so jaded that they feel the only way to put an end to corruption for good is to burn everything to the ground and start afresh . To combine both depictions, sometimes a more sympathetic anarchist/rebel group will be infiltrated by a Psycho Supporter or Mad Bomber type, and manipulated into helping them fulfil their destructive impulses. If the Bomb-Throwing Anarchists have a leader , he will often be frighteningly charismatic and intense, often depicted with excess facial hair , or wearing a mask (à la V ), and they might also be a Queer Rebel in more modern depictions. However, expect the most despicable ones, when push comes to shove, to abandon their loyal followers in an attempt to save their own skin , or turn out to have some kind of ulterior motive . Traditionally equipped with a Cartoon Bomb , described by one stock image-hosting site as an " old-fashioned anarchist-style round bomb with burning fuse ." Modern depictions favor dynamite sticks note Actually sort of accurate; a much-copied innovation of People's Will (who blew up Russian Tsar Alexander II in 1881) was using dynamite as a tool of assassination and Molotov Cocktails . Subtrope of Strawman Political . For their comrades-in-arms , see Eco-Terrorist , Evil Luddite , Malcolm Xerox and Animal Wrongs Group for other militant leftists (usually), prepared to go to extreme lengths to further their goals. Compare and contrast the Revolutionaries Who Don't Do Anything , who are more likely to be found bickering about Anarchist theory and literature than blowing up banks. For the complete opposite to this ideology, see The Evils of Free Will , where a despot seeks to establish absolute order. For actual bomb-throwing as a weapon of choice, see Throw Down the Bomblet . No Real Life Examples, Please!
